items.project_definition: The technical interface exposing v1 endpoints to handle requests and return standardized data envelopes.
Concrete behavioral examples used to validate that the developed logic meets the defined requirements.
A learning offering owned by an instructor, containing pricing in cents, visibility status, and a sequenced set of lessons.
The online learning platform enabling instructional content management and learner progress tracking.
The underlying security mechanism used to store account credentials safely without plaintext exposure.
A relationship record linking a student to a specific offering, capturing completion dates and percentage of progress.
Unique identifiers for specific mandatory system behaviors that must be implemented.
The specific git reference 001-coursehub-api isolating development for this set of requirements.
The requirement to handle pricing as integers in cents to avoid floating-point precision errors.
The international standard applied to the storage and transmission of completion date stamps.
A standalone validation case used to verify a specific workflow from start to finish.
The raw user-provided description used to derive the initial technical specifications.
The lightweight data-interchange format used for all system responses and request bodies.
A child component of a learning offering representing a specific section in a mandatory sequence.
The architectural style using stateless request-response patterns for resource manipulation.
Identifiers for quantifiable benchmarks used to verify successful implementation of behaviors.
A high-level description of a feature from the perspective of a system actor to define a specific value.
A shared account identity containing credentials, a display name, and a role that determines access levels.
